name: Aversa 2017 Tadalafil Lean Mass ED/LUTS Extract
population_or_context: Nonobese men with mild erectile dysfunction and/or lower urinary tract symptoms.
sample_size: 43 men randomized to tadalafil 5 mg once daily or 20 mg on demand.
methods: Two-month randomized comparison of tadalafil dosing patterns, with DXA body composition primary outcome and in vitro skeletal muscle cell assays.
population_profile: Men mean age 48.5 with mild ED and/or LUTS; adjacent to the target age band but selected for symptoms and not healthy athletes.
tadalafil_exposure: Tadalafil 5 mg once daily versus 20 mg on demand for 2 months, followed by withdrawal observation.
indication_context: ED/LUTS clinical population with body-composition and cell-mechanism outcomes.
study_design: Randomized comparison of once-daily versus on-demand tadalafil; no placebo arm reported in the abstract.
comparator_context: On-demand tadalafil active comparison and post-withdrawal return to baseline.
safety_findings: Abstract does not provide detailed adverse-event rates; endothelial and hormonal changes were measured.
directness_to_target_population: Low to moderate for nonobese men near the age band, but low for healthy gym performance because participants had ED/LUTS and no strength or exercise-function endpoint.
confounding_and_bias_limits: No placebo arm in abstract, symptom-selected population, short duration, body composition not function, and in vitro mechanistic extrapolation.
class_transfer_limits: Direct tadalafil evidence, but body-composition and cell findings cannot be treated as proof of strength, hypertrophy, or exercise performance.
